items.question: Explain filter, wrapper, and embedded feature selection with one example each.
items.answer: Filter: rank features with mutual information and keep top k without training a task model. Wrapper: RFE trains logistic regression, drops lowest |coef| features, refits until k remain. Embedded: LassoCV drives unused coefficients to zero during penalized regression training.

items.question: Why must feature selection be fit only on training data inside CV?
items.answer: Selectors use label statistics (MI, supervised RFE, Lasso). If test labels influence which features are kept, information leaks from test into training and validation scores are optimistically biased.

items.question: How does mutual information differ from Pearson correlation as a filter?
items.answer: Pearson measures linear association. Mutual information measures general statistical dependence and can rank non-linearly related features higher, though estimation noise and discrete binning choices affect MI scores.

items.question: When would you choose PCA over feature selection?
items.answer: Choose PCA when features are highly correlated and a small number of orthogonal combinations explains most variance, and interpretability of raw columns matters less. Choose selection when stakeholders must audit named original inputs.
